name: Annie Hall's Butter Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cream together butter and sugar with an electric mixer (or hand-held).
3. Add egg yolks and vanilla, mixing well.
4. Sift flour and salt together, then beat into the mixture until combined.
5. Shape dough into cookies about 1 inch apart on an ungreased cookie sheet.
6. Bake for about 10 minutes, ensuring they do not brown.
7. Remove cookies while still warm and cool on wax paper.
8. Decorate as desired.
Shapes:
- Candy Canes: Divide 3 cups of dough in half. Add red food coloring to one portion. Roll each into a 4-inch strip no wider than a pencil. Press lightly together, twist like a rope, and place cookies about 1 inch apart on an ungreased sheet. Curve the top into a candy cane shape and bake for 10 minutes without browning.
- Rolled Cookies: Shape dough into rolls with cookie thickness. Wrap in wax paper and refrigerate until firm. Roll out to about 1/2 inch, cut shapes if desired, then bake as above.
- Stars, Hearts, Circles, and Jelly-Filled: Pipe chilled dough through a star tip (sizes 2 and 7) onto an ungreased sheet into desired shapes. For jelly-filled cookies, fill the center with red jelly before baking. Decorate as desired and bake for 10 minutes without browning.
- Cookie Cutter Cookies: Chill dough, roll to about 1/2 inch thickness, cut with cookie cutters, place cookies 1 inch apart on an ungreased sheet, and bake for 10 minutes.
- Plain Old Drop Cookies: Drop spoonfuls of chilled dough onto an ungreased sheet, bake at 350°F for 10 minutes without browning.
